title: GOOGL — laggard or not
question: Was Google really a laggard, and what drove the 2025-26 re-rating?
finding: Never the laggard. On clean prices GOOGL returned +148% (2nd of 6, beats 4 of 5 megacap peers, loses only to NVDA); the "laggard" was a 23x data-corruption bug plus benchmark choice. Of five named catalysts only two survive (Gemini 2.0 and DOJ remedies); the "leader-phase alpha" is not significant once the start date is exogenous (Newey-West t=1.15, bootstrap CI crosses 0). The +148% is ~62% earnings / 38% multiple — a delivery story, not a re-rate.
